evals.prompt: I have a blog draft in docs/blogs/drafts/marketing-automation-guide.md. Prep it for publishing.
evals.expected_output: Should read keywords-used/all.jsonl to find the draft_path and keyword metadata. Should read the draft from docs/blogs/drafts/. Should read product-marketing-context.md and keyword-strategy.md (including Blog Post Instructions for blog directory). Should discover the repo structure (Astro or Hydrogen), CTA component, contact page, resources page, and site config. Should verify author info exists in site config. Should copy the draft from docs/blogs/drafts/ into the site's blog directory with a keyword-optimal slug, avoiding duplicates. Should clean up frontmatter to match existing posts including keywords array. Should verify footnote format is Astro-compatible. Should enforce 'we' tone, remove AI slop, run content guardrails. Should add internal links (2 home, 2 pillar, 2 blog, 2 service, 1-2 resources). Should add 'Why {Company}' section with CTA callout. Should add FAQ section with schema. Should add Article schema. Should run final compliance checklist. Should move draft folder from docs/blogs/drafts/ to docs/blogs/published/. Should update keywords-used ledger with final blog_path.
assertions: Reads keywords-used/all.jsonl to find draft_path and keyword metadata
Reads Blog Post Instructions from keyword-strategy.md for blog directory
Verifies author info in site config
Copies draft from drafts/ to blog directory with keyword-optimal slug
Checks slug doesn't duplicate existing URLs
Adds FAQ section with FAQ schema
Adds Article schema with author from config
Updates keywords-used ledger with final blog_path

evals.prompt: publish this blog draft — it mentions our Austin, TX office a lot since it's about local SEO services
evals.expected_output: Should detect geographic content (Austin, TX) and add LocalBusiness schema with areaServed in addition to the standard Article and FAQ schemas. Should ensure the meta description includes the location. Should still add all standard internal links, CTA, FAQ, and run compliance checks. Geographic schema should reference the specific area mentioned in the article.

evals.prompt: integrate this blog post into our site
evals.expected_output: Should trigger on 'integrate' phrasing. Should read keywords-used/all.jsonl for draft paths. Should check docs/blogs/drafts/ for available draft folders. If multiple drafts exist, should list them and ask the user which one. Should read Blog Post Instructions from keyword-strategy.md for the blog directory. Should read existing blog posts to learn the frontmatter format. Should discover the CTA component or offer to create one. Should copy draft from drafts/ to blog directory, then proceed through all steps. Should move the draft folder to published/ at the end.
assertions: Reads keywords-used ledger for draft paths
Asks which draft if multiple exist
Reads Blog Post Instructions from keyword-strategy.md
Reads existing posts to learn patterns
Copies draft from drafts/ to blog directory
Moves draft folder to published/ at the end

evals.prompt: This draft mentions competitor pricing and makes some bold claims about ROI. Can you clean it up and publish it?
evals.expected_output: Should flag the pricing mentions and bold ROI claims during the content guardrails check in Step 3. Should remove specific pricing references. Should soften or remove guaranteed ROI claims (e.g., change 'you will see 300% ROI' to 'many businesses report significant improvements'). Should not publish until these issues are resolved. Should report the specific violations found and the fixes applied. Should still complete all other publish prep steps after cleanup.
assertions: Flags pricing mentions during content guardrails
Softens or removes guaranteed ROI claims
Does not publish with guardrail violations
Completes all other publish prep steps after cleanup

evals.prompt: The blog draft uses 'I think' and 'In my experience' throughout — it reads like a personal blog, not a company post. Fix it and publish.
evals.expected_output: Should detect the 'I' voice issue during the tone check in Step 3. Should convert all 'I' statements to 'we' voice (e.g., 'I think' → 'we believe', 'In my experience' → 'In our experience'). Should scan for all first-person singular pronouns and replace them. Should verify the tone is consistently 'we' after changes. Should still be educational and promotional, not just mechanically replacing pronouns.
assertions: Replaces 'In my experience' with 'In our experience'
Scans for all first-person singular pronouns

evals.prompt: Publish this draft. Our site doesn't have a CTA component yet.
evals.expected_output: Should discover that no CTA component exists during repo structure analysis. Should create a CTA component that matches the site's existing design system (CSS variables, component conventions). For Astro, should create a .astro component with heading, description, buttonText, and buttonUrl props. Should use the new component in the 'Why {Company}' section. Should inform the user that a new component was created and where it lives.
assertions: Creates CTA component matching design system
Uses existing CSS variables and conventions
Includes heading, description, buttonText, buttonUrl props
Informs user about newly created component

evals.prompt: The draft has markdown footnotes like [^1] but we're using Astro with MDX. Will this work?
evals.expected_output: Should check astro.config.mjs for remark plugin configuration. If remarkGfm or a footnotes plugin is configured, should confirm footnotes will work. If no footnote plugin is configured, should convert footnotes to Astro-compatible format: either inline links or a manual 'Sources' list with numbered <sup> tags and anchor links. Should warn that bare [^1] syntax in .mdx files can cause build errors without a plugin. Should match whatever citation format existing posts use.
assertions: Identifies whether footnote plugins are configured
Converts footnotes if no plugin exists
Warns about MDX build errors with bare footnote syntax
Matches existing citation format from other posts

evals.prompt: Publish this blog but some of the service page links in keyword-strategy.md point to pages that don't exist anymore.
evals.expected_output: Should validate service page URLs from keyword-strategy.md against actual pages in the src/ directory during Step 4. Should flag the broken/missing service pages to the user. Should ask the user to update keyword-strategy.md with correct URLs. Should not link to nonexistent pages. Should still complete linking for service pages that do exist. Should use alternative relevant pages if the user provides them.
assertions: Validates service page URLs against src/ directory
Flags missing service pages to user
Does not link to nonexistent pages
Completes linking for valid service pages
